"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be aware of the potential risks associated with such content. As a retired 86-year-old man living in Bari, Italy, I have seen how fake news can spread quickly and cause confusion among people. To avoid falling prey to such content, I recommend using fact-checking websites and verifying the authenticity of information before sharing it on social media. Additionally, I suggest following reputable news sources and using privacy settings to limit the amount of misinformation that appears in your feed. By taking these steps, you can help protect yourself and others from the negative effects of fake content on social media.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is essential to be aware of the potential risks associated with such content. As a retired 86-year-old man living in Bari, Italy, I have seen how fake news can spread quickly and cause confusion among people. To avoid falling prey to such content, I recommend using fact-checking websites and verifying the authenticity of information before sharing it on social media. Additionally, I suggest following reputable news sources and using privacy settings to limit the amount of misinformation that appears in your feed. By taking these steps, you can help protect yourself and others from the negative effects of fake content on social media.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is crucial to be aware of the potential risks associated with such content. As a retired 86-year-old man living in Bari, Italy, I have seen how fake news can spread quickly and cause confusion among people. By using fact-checking websites, verifying the authenticity of information before sharing it on social media, following reputable news sources, and using privacy settings to limit the amount of misinformation that appears in your feed, you can help protect yourself and others from the negative effects of fake content on social media.
